description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the michaeltlombardi/documentarian Docker image and defines three stages: build, test, and deploy, with a global before_script that makes ./build.ps1 and ./psake.ps1 executable (chmod +x). In the build stage add a build_module job that runs `pwsh` followed by `./build.ps1 -TaskList BuildModule` and publishes the generated `documentarian` directory as an artifact named documentarian. In the test stage add a test_documentation job that runs `pwsh` and `./build.ps1 -TaskList GenerateBoth`, stores `documentarian.pdf` and the `public` folder as artifacts, and is excluded from the master branch. In the deploy stage add a pages job that runs `./build.ps1 -TaskList GenerateSite`, publishes the `public` directory as a GitLab Pages artifact, and runs only on the master branch, and a pdf job that runs `pwsh` then `./build.ps1 -TaskList GeneratePdf`, saving `documentarian.pdf` as an artifact, also only on master. Finally, add a publish_module job that runs `pwsh` and `./build.ps1 -TaskList PublishModule -ApiKey $ENV:gallerykey`, and is triggered only for tags matching the pattern vX.Y.Z or any tag in the documentarian/documentian project.
